Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Micah Haroldson

May 3rd, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Pinnacle Wealth Management Advisory Group LLC raised its position in PepsiCo, Inc. ( NASDAQ:PEP - Free Report) by 54.7% during the 4th quarter, according to its most recent disclosure with the SEC. The fund owned 37,386 shares of the company's stock after purchasing an additional 13,214 shares during the period. Pinnacle Wealth Management Advisory Group LLC's holdings in PepsiCo were worth $5,366,000 at the end of the most recent reporting period.

Other large investors also recently bought and sold shares of the company. Captrust Financial Advisors grew its holdings in PepsiCo by 3.6% in the third quarter. Captrust Financial Advisors now owns 554,798 shares of the company's stock valued at $77,916,000 after purchasing an additional 19,218 shares during the last quarter. Murphy Middleton Hinkle & Parker Inc. boosted its stake in shares of PepsiCo by 622.3% during the fourth quarter. Murphy Middleton Hinkle & Parker Inc. now owns 35,603 shares of the company's stock valued at $5,110,000 after acquiring an additional 30,674 shares during the last quarter. Kathmere Capital Management LLC boosted its stake in shares of PepsiCo by 122.6% during the third quarter. Kathmere Capital Management LLC now owns 20,365 shares of the company's stock valued at $2,860,000 after acquiring an additional 11,217 shares during the last quarter. Mediolanum International Funds Ltd boosted its stake in shares of PepsiCo by 19.3% during the third quarter. Mediolanum International Funds Ltd now owns 252,916 shares of the company's stock valued at $35,451,000 after acquiring an additional 40,947 shares during the last quarter. Finally, Townsquare Capital LLC boosted its stake in shares of PepsiCo by 96.5% during the third quarter. Townsquare Capital LLC now owns 40,383 shares of the company's stock valued at $5,671,000 after acquiring an additional 19,829 shares during the last quarter. Institutional investors and hedge funds own 73.07% of the company's stock.

Key Stories Impacting PepsiCo

Here are the key news stories impacting PepsiCo this week:

Positive Sentiment: PepsiCo signed a 10-year virtual power purchase agreement with Statkraft to cut ~32,000 metric tons of CO2 annually across Europe - supports sustainability targets and may lower long-term energy costs. PepsiCo inks 10-year renewable energy deal to cut emissions across Europe

Positive Sentiment: Zacks Research raised multiple near- and medium-term EPS estimates (including FY2027 and FY2028), signaling improved analyst expectations for profitability. PepsiCo (PEP) analyst note (MarketBeat summary)

Positive Sentiment: PepsiCo is expanding electrification of distribution with a 50 electric delivery truck rollout in Fresno - incremental cost and emissions benefits and supports supply-chain decarbonization narrative. Fresno PepsiCo rolls out 50 electric delivery trucks, aims to reduce emissions

Positive Sentiment: Company announced a 2026 growth plan focused on cost cuts and product innovation - potential margin improvement and revenue growth tailwinds if execution holds. PepsiCo sets 2026 growth plan with cost cuts, innovation push

Neutral Sentiment: Analyst and media pieces comparing Coca-Cola vs. PepsiCo provide context for relative valuation and may drive short-term trading but contain no new company-specific catalysts. Coca-Cola vs. PepsiCo: Which Stock Is the Better Buy?

Neutral Sentiment: PepsiCo plans to end its bottling/distribution partnership with Royal Unibrew in parts of Northern Europe by 2028 - strategic operational change that will require new local arrangements (potential short-term costs, long-term control). PepsiCo Bottling Shift In Northern Europe And What It Means For Valuation

Negative Sentiment: PepsiCo apologised to South African consumers and pulled its ProNutro formula from shelves after a backlash - immediate reputational damage, potential regulatory scrutiny and lost sales in the market; this appears to be the most direct near-term negative catalyst. PepsiCo apologises to South Africans and pulls ProNutro from shelves over formula backlash

Negative Sentiment: PepsiCo is named in a consumer lawsuit alleging ultraprocessed foods can be addictive - legal risk and reputational headlines that could pressure longer-term consumer perception and marketing costs. Food giants hit with consumer lawsuit calling ultraprocessed ingredients addictive

Negative Sentiment: Erste Group trimmed FY2026/27 EPS estimates slightly - a small downward analyst revision that marginally reduces near-term analyst sentiment. Erste Group Bank analyst note (MarketBeat summary)

Wall Street Analyst Weigh In

A number of analysts recently commented on PEP shares. TD Cowen increased their price objective on PepsiCo from $162.00 to $165.00 and gave the company a "hold" rating in a research note on Friday, February 20th. Morgan Stanley increased their price objective on PepsiCo from $165.00 to $180.00 and gave the company an "equal weight" rating in a research note on Wednesday, February 4th. Royal Bank Of Canada decreased their price objective on PepsiCo from $165.00 to $163.00 and set a "sector perform" rating on the stock in a research note on Thursday, April 9th. Rothschild & Co Redburn increased their price objective on PepsiCo from $120.00 to $130.00 and gave the company a "sell" rating in a research note on Tuesday, February 10th. Finally, Evercore increased their price objective on PepsiCo from $165.00 to $170.00 in a research note on Friday, April 17th. Eight analysts have rated the stock with a Buy rating, eleven have assigned a Hold rating and one has assigned a Sell rating to the company. According to data from MarketBeat, the stock currently has an average rating of "Hold" and an average target price of $170.26.

Check Out Our Latest Analysis on PepsiCo

PepsiCo Stock Performance

NASDAQPEP opened at $157.39 on Monday. The business's 50-day moving average price is $157.96 and its 200 day moving average price is $152.43. PepsiCo, Inc. has a twelve month low of $127.60 and a twelve month high of $171.48. The company has a market capitalization of $215.11 billion, a PE ratio of 24.71, a price-to-earnings-growth ratio of 2.88 and a beta of 0.38. The company has a current ratio of 0.90, a quick ratio of 0.72 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.98.

PepsiCo ( NASDAQ:PEP - Get Free Report) last released its earnings results on Thursday, April 16th. The company reported $1.61 earnings per share for the quarter, topping the consensus estimate of $1.55 by $0.06. PepsiCo had a return on equity of 56.61% and a net margin of 9.15%.The firm had revenue of $19.44 billion for the quarter, compared to the consensus estimate of $18.89 billion. During the same period in the prior year, the business posted $1.48 earnings per share. The firm's revenue for the quarter was up 8.5% compared to the same quarter last year. PepsiCo has set its FY 2026 guidance at 8.465-8.628 EPS. On average, research analysts predict that PepsiCo, Inc. will post 8.63 earnings per share for the current year.

PepsiCo Announces Dividend

The firm also recently announced a quarterly dividend, which was paid on Tuesday, March 31st. Investors of record on Friday, March 6th were given a dividend of $1.4225 per share. The ex-dividend date was Friday, March 6th. This represents a $5.69 annualized dividend and a yield of 3.6%. PepsiCo's dividend payout ratio is 89.32%.

PepsiCo declared that its board has authorized a stock buyback program on Tuesday, February 3rd that allows the company to buyback $10.00 billion in outstanding shares. This buyback authorization allows the company to purchase up to 4.7% of its stock through open market purchases. Stock buyback programs are usually a sign that the company's management believes its stock is undervalued.

About PepsiCo

( Free Report)

PepsiCo, Inc (NASDAQ: PEP) is a multinational food and beverage company headquartered in Purchase, New York. The company develops, manufactures, markets and sells a broad portfolio of branded food and beverage products, including carbonated and noncarbonated soft drinks, bottled water, sports drinks, juices, ready-to-drink teas and coffees, salty snacks, cereals, and other convenient foods. Its leading consumer brands include Pepsi, Mountain Dew, Gatorade, Tropicana, Quaker, Lay's, Doritos and Cheetos, among others.

Formed through the 1965 merger of Pepsi-Cola and Frito-Lay, PepsiCo has grown into a global business with integrated manufacturing, distribution and marketing operations.

Want to see what other hedge funds are holding PEP? Visit HoldingsChannel.com to get the latest 13F filings and insider trades for PepsiCo, Inc. ( NASDAQ:PEP - Free Report).

urn:contentItem:6JJ7-P0T3-S31K-S1X1-00000-00May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: SWINERTON INCORPORATED & PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: SWINERTON INCORPORATED & PEER INSIGHTS

Swinerton Incorporated remained active across public infrastructure, mission critical construction, and regional leadership development during 2026. On April 16, 2026, Swinerton Builders was selected by San Francisco Public Works as the construction manager/general contractor for a new $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility. The project, located in the city's southeastern district, is designed to modernize emergency response training with a 50,000-square-foot scenario district replicating San Francisco neighborhood conditions, including sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and post-earthquake structural simulations. The campus is also set to include emergency medical services training, onsite fire apparatus instruction, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions. The administration and classroom building is planned as an Essential Services Building for immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and as a backup emergency operations center. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager for Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, said the center would equip first responders with critical training resources, while project executive Christina Pantera said the company would apply experience from more than 250 San Francisco projects, including over 40 public safety facilities, to deliver the site in coordination with city stakeholders and community members.

Earlier in the month, on April 8, 2026, Swinerton announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, a move tied to the company's expansion in data center and mission critical infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of experience in complex project delivery and has previously launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center construction division, as well as managed strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program. In the role, she is expected to lead Swinerton's mission critical strategy with an emphasis on speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, chief revenue officer, said her appointment would strengthen growth in the sector and improve client experience. The company also pointed to her expertise in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration as relevant to evolving client demand.

That expansion theme continued on March 16, 2026, when Swinerton Builders announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division. Jay Quackenbush was promoted to vice president and division manager, with Nicholas Schifferle joining as director of client relations and Robert Murphy taking on the role of project executive. Quackenbush brings nearly 30 years of construction experience and longstanding familiarity with New York City building conditions. Ray Haj, chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, said the promotion would improve the division's ability to meet a broad range of client needs, from facility management to large ground-up assignments. Schifferle added more than 20 years of international business development experience spanning Asia, Australia, and the United States, while Murphy arrived with oversight experience on more than $650 million in commercial interiors work. Since entering the Northeast market in 2021, the division has completed office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up projects across all five boroughs and has continued expanding into education, residential, and public work.

Across the peer group, Balfour Beatty reported a combination of operational expansion and financial momentum. On April 1, 2026, the company promoted Trent Johnson to vice president of operations in the Carolinas, with responsibility for leading and growing its aviation sector in North and South Carolina. Johnson has more than 19 years of construction experience, including over 12 years with Balfour Beatty, during which he contributed to 22 aviation projects ranging from targeted renovations to multi-phase programs. His work has included projects at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. Jeff Sandeen, president of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, said Johnson had built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner in local aviation operations and credited his ability to guide teams through active airport environments as a key driver of regional success.

Financially, Balfour Beatty highlighted growth supported by a record order book. On March 11, 2026, the company said market valuation had risen by nearly two-thirds over the previous year, with underlying earnings per share up 9% to 47.6 pence. Operating profit in support services rose by one-third to £122 million, while underlying margin in that segment improved by 80 basis points to 8.5%. The company said growth was supported by power transmission demand in the UK and higher building volumes in the US, although cost overruns in one US civils project remained a factor. It also said its UK construction division achieved margin targets ahead of schedule, reflecting improved operational performance.

Hensel Phelps was linked to one of the more visible construction technology announcements in the group through Track3D's expansion across its portfolio. On April 1, 2026, Track3D said Hensel Phelps had entered a multi-year agreement to deploy its Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, extending a relationship that began with a single pilot. The rollout is intended to standardize progress monitoring across the contractor's portfolio and improve early issue detection to reduce delays and improve schedule reliability. A case study at San Francisco International Airport was cited as evidence of the platform's ability to shift project teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and use of existing field staff. Track3D also used the update to highlight new enterprise features, including an admin dashboard, groups functionality, and capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, all aimed at large contractor oversight and workflow efficiency.

Hensel Phelps also made a leadership move in Hawaii. On April 1, 2026, Micah "Pono" Choy was promoted to project superintendent on the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. Choy joined the company in 2018 and has contributed to aviation, wastewater, and federal projects in Hawaii and Guam. The company said the promotion reflected both project experience and a collaborative leadership style that has helped build strong partnerships across teams.

Quanta Services used acquisition activity and investor messaging to underline its positioning in utility, renewable, and energy infrastructure. On April 6, 2026, the company completed its acquisition of Davenport-based Tri-City Group, a contractor specializing in electrical construction and industrial services. Tri-City said it would continue operating under its existing name and brand, and Lori Rochholz, director of marketing, said customers would continue to see the same leadership and responsiveness, now with access to Quanta's broader resources. Quanta also referenced additional acquisitions including Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service. President and chief executive officer Duke Austin said the acquisitions expanded the company's craft-skilled platform to support critical electric utility programs across the United States. Tri-City, which traces its roots to 1895 and has worked across 46 states and multiple countries, remains active through subsidiaries including Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy.

Before that, on March 3, 2026, Quanta Services used the Jefferies Power, Utilities, & Clean Energy Conference to outline its growth outlook for 2026. Management pointed to clean energy demand, infrastructure development, data center market opportunities, and relationships with midcap utilities such as NIPSCO as part of its strategic direction. The company said it remained focused on organic growth, disciplined capital allocation, and strategic acquisitions, while presenting a framework centered on long-term value creation and risk management.

Barton Malow's recent updates reflected continued involvement in sports and civic infrastructure. On April 28, 2026, the company was named construction manager for Detroit City FC's new 15,000-seat stadium, AlumniFi Field, on the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital. The stadium is scheduled to open in spring 2027 and is intended to address demand beyond the club's current 7,000-seat Keyworth Stadium, where attendance has regularly exceeded capacity. The venue is planned between Corktown and Mexicantown and will include a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and space for community events in addition to soccer matches. Senior vice president Sean Hollister said the project aligned with Barton Malow's commitment to technical excellence and community engagement, while Detroit City FC chief executive officer Sean Mann said the stadium would reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit.

Barton Malow also joined March-Westin as construction manager for the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project at West Virginia University, with work set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture is responsible for preconstruction planning, procurement, schedule and cost management, and onsite coordination. Regional executive Scott Mull said the company would bring the same focus and craftsmanship it has applied to athletic facilities nationwide, while March-Westin president Jamie Ridgeway said the combination of local knowledge and national stadium experience would support delivery with minimal disruption to fans.

DPR Construction's updates centered on talent, office growth, and workplace recognition. On April 29, 2026, the company said it would expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet with local support from Henrico County. The additional space is intended to support workforce growth, improve collaboration, and strengthen operational capacity in the region. DPR said the expanded office would incorporate modern design features and sustainable practices, with completion expected within the following year.

A month earlier, on April 1, 2026, DPR appointed Jason Page to lead its Nashville Special Services Group. Page has more than 14 years of experience and has been with DPR since 2012. Since relocating to Nashville in 2021, he has led corporate interior and entertainment assignments under tight deadlines. The company said he would oversee a group responsible for a broad mix of work, from targeted improvements to major interior buildouts, while also continuing to mentor project teams and share best practices for high-impact, quick-turn delivery.

On March 31, 2026, DPR also announced Great Place To Work Certification, with 97% of employees saying it is a great place to work, a score the company said was 40 points above the average US workplace benchmark. Chief executive officer George Pfeffer said the certification was especially meaningful because it came directly from employees and reflected a culture of trust, support, and growth. DPR linked the recognition to broader learning and development programs and to a decentralized operating model that encourages initiative and open information sharing across its workforce of more than 11,000 professionals.

Turner Construction's recent news flow was led by large-scale data center, healthcare, and mixed-use assignments. On April 28, 2026, the company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, completing the structural phase of a 72-megawatt facility less than five months after groundbreaking. The 450,000-square-foot building sits on a 75-acre campus and includes a two-story structural steel design, insulated precast exterior system, and hybrid cooling technology combining liquid and air-cooled systems. Turner said more than 80,000 craft hours had been logged to date, with around 345 skilled workers contributing. Operations manager Nick Sevier said the milestone reflected the strength of Turner's partnership with Aligned, the dedication of trade partners, and the company's commitment to delivering a complex, high-performance facility safely and efficiently.

Also on April 28, 2026, Turner said vertical construction had begun on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower is expected to expand bed count from 356 to 500, increase operating rooms from 12 to 18, enlarge the emergency department, and support the health system's private-room strategy. Turner said the project is expected to generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact, support up to 3,800 construction jobs, and expand onsite labor from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak periods. Vice president and senior general manager David Kaminski said the project would expand access to modern healthcare while supporting caregivers and the wider community.

PCL Construction combined culture, infrastructure, and digital capacity themes in its announcements. On April 2, 2026, the company said it had been named to the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list for the 17th time, ranking No. 91. Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of US operations, said the employee-owned structure helped shape a culture in which people feel trusted, supported, and invested in the future. The company linked the recognition to its standing as No. 11 on Engineering News-Record's Top 400 Contractors list for 2025 and to its track record delivering complex projects across North America.

PCL also highlighted progress on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center near Denver. On March 19, 2026, PCL Construction Services and PCL Industrial Services announced structural completion of the AI-ready facility, which spans approximately 277,000 square feet across five steel-frame colocation halls on a 17-acre site. The center is designed for enterprise-scale artificial intelligence and graphics processing workloads and includes a separate administration building. PCL Industrial Services is self-performing core electrical work, including power systems integration, while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems. Vice president and district manager Ryan Schmidt said the company's mission critical and data center experience remained central to executing high-density projects of this type. Construction is scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

JE Dunn Construction focused its recent updates on manufacturing strategy, workforce development, and sector leadership. On April 6, 2026, the company launched Form Off-Site Solutions, a new initiative specializing in design for manufacturing for complex construction components. Led by off-site manufacturing director Nick Effenheim, the operation offers metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies. JE Dunn said the model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, project safety, quality, and cost certainty while helping address labor shortages and schedule pressure. Effenheim said the ability to move ideas and concepts into a manufactured environment was a differentiator, while president and chief executive officer Gordon Lansford described the business as a step forward in the company's effort to drive innovation for clients.

On March 19, 2026, JE Dunn also celebrated the topping out of the Northland Workforce Development Center in Kansas City, Missouri. The 145,000-square-foot, two-story project is intended to expand access to career and technical education in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. The milestone included a beam-signing ceremony involving the Platte County R-3 School District and other stakeholders. Project executive Brad Hokanson said the project was about creating pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals, while Brian Noller of the Platte County School District said the initiative represented a long-term regional investment supported by state, municipal, business, and community partners. Substantial completion is targeted for 2027.

McCarthy Holdings paired healthcare execution with sustainability reporting in its recent releases. On February 6, 2026, the company reached topping out on the $3.7 billion California Tower at UC Davis Health, part of an eight-year Vision 2030 expansion plan on the Sacramento campus. The 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion are expected to add approximately 334 inpatient beds along with operating rooms, imaging, pharmacy, and burn care facilities. UC Davis Health said the opening is expected in 2030. McCarthy noted that the tower is the final component of a plan intended to expand the campus from 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million square feet.

McCarthy then issued its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas Emissions Report on February 16, 2026. Developed with Gravity, the report covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and selected Scope 3 categories, with Scope 1 and Scope 2 results independently audited and awarded limited assurance. Senior director of sustainability Ian Pope said the work provided teams with practical data to meet regulatory requirements and aligned with the company's employee-owned structure and core values. McCarthy also highlighted biodiversity initiatives, fuel management efforts, use of digital tools and artificial intelligence in decision-making, and a photovoltaic recycling program using Solar Energy Industries Association-approved recyclers.

Clark Construction Group's recent news included a senior HR appointment. On April 29, 2026, Carly Reiner Caceres joined as director of human resources for national campus recruiting. The company said her background across people operations, talent strategy, employer branding, and full-cycle recruiting would support early-career talent pipelines nationally. Before joining Clark, she held leadership roles at Proper, MILLER, Accenture, and Grant Thornton. The appointment comes as Clark continues to invest in long-term talent development across its building and infrastructure operations.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RV86-D4YS-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Sets Out 2026 Organizational Structure Under Musk's Leadership2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Sets Out 2026 Organizational Structure Under Musk's Leadership

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 15:00 EDT

As of May 5th, 2026, Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) operates with a functional organizational model that integrates hierarchical elements to support rapid decision-making and innovation. Central decision-making rests with the executive team at the Austin, Texas headquarters, with Musk among the top decision-makers. The structure centers on functional hubs for finance, sales, marketing, engineering, and design, enabling coordinated operations across product lines and faster development cycles.

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) differs from traditional automakers by maintaining stronger central governance rather than broad regional autonomy. The design prioritizes consistency in strategy and execution, aided by streamlined channels. It is not strictly hierarchical, reflecting Musk's preference for lean bureaucracy that encourages cross-department collaboration and, at times, bypassing formal processes to spur innovation.

These choices influence market responsiveness and brand consistency. The centralized approach accelerates decisions but may hinder adaptability in diverse markets. While Toyota's hybrid model offers a balance between central control and local flexibility, Tesla's ongoing focus on innovation, including AI-driven initiatives, keeps it at the forefront of the automotive and energy sectors. The 2026 structure underscores Elon Musk's influence on the company's strategic direction as it seeks to maintain leadership in its fields.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

NYSE END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

SAN DIEGO, Thursday, May 14 2026 - Sempra's (NYSE: SRE $92.86) stock price is up 1.3% after an upgrade in the past week. It was upgraded to Outperform by the analyst at BMO Capital Markets.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 61.5 points (0.8%) in the day, the relative price increase was 0.5%.

There were 2,810,024 shares worth $260.9 million traded today; the volume was 0.8 times the average daily trading volume of 3.7 million shares. Since November 24, Sempra has had eighteen analyst rating changes with eleven being upgrades. Sempra is up 0.3% since November 24. Out of the 18 analysts we track, eleven believe the stock price will go higher. The stock price is currently at a premium of 1% of the lowest analyst target price and at a discount of 19% of the highest analyst target price.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 372 words

Mon May 11, 2026: Sempra Moves Closer to Initial LNG Production at ECA Terminal

Sempra is on the brink of initiating LNG production at its Energ a Costa Azul (ECA) facility in Mexico this June, marking a significant stride in its energy infrastructure expansion. As of the 11th of May 2026, the facility has begun receiving feed gas as of April 20, positioning itself for full operations at a capacity of 3.25 million tons per annum, according to Industrial Info Resources. This development enhances Sempra's competitive edge in the North American LNG export landscape, with gas sourced primarily from the US Permian Basin.

The company's first-quarter 2026 earnings will be disclosed on May 7, providing investors with a clearer picture of Sempra's financial performance in light of these operational advancements. The ongoing progress at the ECA terminal is a crucial element of Sempra's strategy to bolster its LNG export capabilities, which are vital as global demand for LNG continues to surge due to energy transition initiatives worldwide.

Sempra has established itself as a leading player in North American energy infrastructure, focusing on both utilities and LNG development. Its diversified portfolio includes regulated utility operations in California and Texas, complemented by the growing importance of LNG projects like ECA. The company's emphasis on infrastructure investment aims to meet the increasing energy demands while ensuring safety and operational excellence, as outlined in its investor communications.

The LNG sector is witnessing heightened demand driven by global energy transitions, positioning Sempra favorably as it capitalizes on its US-Mexico gateway. The strategic location of the ECA terminal on the Pacific Coast offers logistical advantages for exports to Asian markets, distinguishing it from competitors based on the Gulf Coast. Furthermore, Sempra's significant operations in Texas link it closely to the overall economic growth of the United States, solidifying its role in the energy sector.

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

May 7, 2026: AEP's $78 Billion Capital Plan Validates Texas Hyperscale Thesis, Strengthening Sempra's Oncor Growth Outlook

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on Sempra: SRE

Following this news, Sempra faces validation of its Texas-centric infrastructure thesis as AEP highlights ERCOT's 41 GW of contracted load commitments and the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The bill is expected to provide greater certainty around interconnection timing for new load growth, a development that benefits Sempra's Oncor subsidiary, the largest transmission and distribution utility in Texas by customer count. Oncor's role in delivering power to data center, industrial, and residential customers across north and westTexas places Sempra in direct economic alignment with AEP Texas's load growth, since both utilities depend on shared ERCOT transmission planning, generation interconnection queues, and supply chain capacity.

Sempra's California utilities and LNG infrastructure businesses also benefit from the same secular electrification and AI-driven demand trends. Strategically, AEP's disclosure provides Sempra additional regulatory and investor support for its own multi-year Texas-led capital expansion.

value.Title: Bloom Energy Corp ( NYSE :BE) Named Zacks Bull of the Day Amid AI Power Demand

urn:contentItem:6JHD-FJP3-S05G-N467-00000-00Bloom Energy Corp (NYSE:BE) Named Zacks Bull of the Day Amid AI Power Demand2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisBloom Energy Corp (NYSE:BE) Named Zacks Bull of the Day Amid AI Power Demand

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

05 May 2026 12:01 EDT

Bloom Energy Corp (NYSE:BE) earns Zacks Bull of the Day designation after a stock rally and rising profile in clean energy deployments. On 30th April 2026, Zacks highlighted Bloom Energy as a key player amid growing demand for energy solutions driven by the expanding AI sector.

Bloom Energy designs solid-oxide fuel cell systems that generate electricity on-site, delivering a cleaner alternative to conventional power generation. As AI workloads rise, data centers will require reliable and efficient energy sources, a need Bloom Energy seeks to address with its on-site energy servers.

Analysts point to a large addressable market as AI related CAPEX accelerates from $390 billion to $674 billion year over year. A key catalyst is Bloom Energy's expanded partnership with Oracle to deploy fuel cell solutions for AI cloud and infrastructure. Following a strong earnings report that beat expectations, Bloom Energy's stock moved higher, while KB Home was named Bear of the Day due to high rates and softer earnings

Additionally, Bloom Energy is expanding its partnerships for hydrogen-based applications, positioning the company for continued growth in the evolving clean energy market through strategic technological advancements and commercialization efforts. It is the NYSE's 2nd largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 4, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Sun May 10, 2026: Sempra Shares Climb Following $10 Billion Infrastructure Stake Sale

Sempra's stock price surged nearly 5% on the New York Stock Exchange following the announcement of a significant divestiture. On 10th May 2026, the San Diego-based utility holding company revealed it would sell a 45% interest in its infrastructure business to private equity firm KKR for $10 billion. This strategic move is aimed at simplifying Sempra's business model and enhancing its financial position.

The infrastructure unit being sold includes operations related to liquefied natural gas terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra's chairman and CEO, emphasized that the transaction is designed to streamline operations and fund robust utility growth in key markets like Texas and California. He stated that this sale underscores Sempra's commitment to a long-term partnership with KKR, aligning their vision to strengthen America's global leadership in LNG exports.

Upon completion of the deal, a KKR-led consortium will hold a 65% equity stake in Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra will maintain a 25% interest and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority will acquire the remaining 10%. Sempra indicated that the transaction will result in approximately 95% of its earnings coming from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This sale marks a pivotal shift in Sempra's strategic focus, allowing the company to concentrate on its core utility operations while simultaneously bolstering its financial strength. The transaction is expected to enhance Sempra's ability to invest in its regulated utility businesses, which are crucial for growth in the evolving energy landscape. Analysts are closely watching how this move will impact Sempra's long-term growth trajectory and market positioning.

Investors are encouraged by the news, as the stock's upward movement reflects confidence in Sempra's strategic direction. The collaboration with KKR, a well-established player in the private equity space, could open new avenues for future investments and operational efficiencies. As Sempra continues to navigate the complexities of the energy market, this transaction may serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation in the utility sector.

Recent News 2 of 4, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 239 words

Sat May 9, 2026: Sempra Targets 11% Utility Rate Base Growth by 2030

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company, is embarking on a strategic simplification of its business model to prioritize utility operations. This shift is expected to drive an 11% annual rate base growth through the year 2030, as announced on 11th May 2026.

A key factor contributing to Sempra's recent performance is the Oncor base rate review settlement. This settlement establishes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5% and a return on equity (ROE) of 9.75%, which aligns rates more closely with the current cost environment, thereby enhancing profitability.

Texas has emerged as a focal point for infrastructure development, driven by surging demand from artificial intelligence and data centers. In response, Sempra has initiated a record $65 billion capital plan, with a significant portion of the rate base expected to be concentrated in Texas by 2030, positioning the company favorably for future growth.

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

value.Title: STIFEL LAUNCHES PROJECT FINANCE OFFERING TO EXPAND ENERGY AND DEEP TECH CAPABILITIES
value.Overview: Contify Investment News, 561words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-9RJ3-S8JG-N0PN-00000-00STIFEL LAUNCHES PROJECT FINANCE OFFERING TO EXPAND ENERGY AND DEEP TECH CAPABILITIES2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisSTIFEL LAUNCHES PROJECT FINANCE OFFERING TO EXPAND ENERGY AND DEEP TECH CAPABILITIES

Key Highlights:

* New offering aims to support energy and deep tech projects with tailored financing solutions.

* Stifel's expanded capabilities will cater to growing demand in energy and deep tech sectors.

Original Press Release:

ST. LOUIS, May 5 -- Stifel Financial Corp issued the following news release:

- Industry Veterans Bret Turner & Sayoji Goli Join to Lead New Effort

Stifel Financial Corp. (NYSE: SF) today announced the launch of a Project Finance platform to support the development of energy and infrastructure sectors and bridge the transition financing gap. The initiative is led by newly appointed Managing Directors Bret Turner and Sayoji Goli.

Turner and Goli join Stifel from First Citizens Bank and bring over a decade of collaboration and a proven track record of advancing emerging technologies and business models. Turner previously founded and scaled the project finance platform at Silicon Valley Bank, building it into one of the leading renewable energy lending businesses in the United States prior to its acquisition by First Citizens Bank.

Stifel's Project Finance platform is designed to support companies and sponsors scale by financing proven commercial technologies in established and emerging infrastructure assets with strong credit profiles. The Project Finance product expansion compliments the recent addition of Energy Tech and established Deep Tech teams supporting the next evolution of global energy production and consumption. The offering includes a range of financing solutions, including development loans, construction-to-term loans, interest rate swaps, depository services, and investment banking advisory.

"These key hires significantly enhance Stifel's ability to finance infrastructure across the energy and industrial sectors," said Chris Reichert, Stifel Bank CEO. "Their addition supports Stifel's differentiated approach of integrating Project Finance with our Venture Banking, Fund Banking, and Capital Markets capabilities, enabling support for investors and portfolio companies from early-stage development through commercialization and monetization."

"The need for reliable, affordable, and clean power, driven by rapid electrification and digitalization, is creating significant demand for new infrastructure needs that will require vast amounts of capital," said Turner. "This platform allows us to bring our experience in structuring and executing, across both established and emerging technologies, to the Stifel client base."

Stifel Company Information

Stifel Financial Corp. (NYSE: SF) is a diversified financial services firm providing wealth management, commercial and investment banking, trading, and research services to individuals, institutions, and municipalities. Founded in 1890 and headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, the firm operates more than 400 offices across the United States and in major global financial centers. As a firm where success meets success, Stifel works closely with retail and institutional clients aiming to transform opportunities into achievement.

Stifel Bank and Stifel Bank & Trust, Members FDIC, offer a full range of consumer and commercial lending solutions.

To learn more about Stifel, please visit the Company's website at www.stifel.com. For global disclosures, please visit www.stifel.com/investor-relations/press-releases.

[Category: Energy]

Source: Stifel Financial Corp

urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHSN-V49K-00000-00Past Week: Sempra (NYSE:SRE) shares post worst week in three weeks; -$1.99 [-2.1%]2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:05ZLexisNexisPast Week: Sempra (NYSE:SRE) shares post worst week in three weeks; -$1.99 [-2.1%]

NYSE END-OF-WEEK STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Sempra (NYSE:SRE $91.68), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has dipped $1.99 (or 2.1%) in the past week to close at $91.68. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -3.2%.

The shares had their worst week in three weeks since the week-ended Apr 29, 2026.

The price ranged between a low of $91.68 on Wednesday May 13 and a high of $93.40 on Tuesday May 12. In the past week the market cap has declined by $1.3 billion to $59.9 billion.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

Financial News

Quarterly Report (Q4/2025):

Release Date: February 26, 2026

Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 December 2025: Sempra reports 357% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit

Sempra (NYSE:SRE), announced net profit of $352m for the quarter-ended 31 December 2025 [Q4/2025], up 357% from the previous quarter [Q3/2025] and down 47% from the year-earlier period [Q4/2024]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 350% sequentially from 12.0c in Q3/2025 to 54.0c in Q4/2025.

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Dec 2025)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q4/2024], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 48.6%, Revenue was down 0.2% and Net Profit was down 47.1%.

Today's update from Commodities markets - Natural Gas

Natural Gas price has surged 17.60c (or 6.4%) from its last trading session of May 08 to close at $2.93 per mmbtu.

PV$1000 [10 Years] = US$1,398

Present Value of US$1,000 invested in Natural Gas 10 years ago is now worth US$1,398; this corresponds to a TRS or CAGR of 3.4% per annum [Total Annualized Return of Natural Gas.]

52-Week Price Range: $2.5 - $6.83

Wednesday's price drop

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) drops 1.8%, underperforming 82% of the market

SAN DIEGO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Sempra's (NYSE: SRE $91.70) stock price dropped $1.70 (1.8%) from Tuesday's close of $91.70. SECTION 7 CORPORATE PROFILE

7.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

SAN FRANCISCO, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - PG&E Corporation's (NYSE: PCG $16.61) stock price dropped 20.0c (1.2%) from its previous trading session to close at $16.61.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 55.0 points (0.7%) in the day, the relative price change was -1.9%.

In the NYSE market of 1,849 stocks and 32 units traded today, the stock has a 1-month relative price strength of 10 indicating it has underperformed 90% of the market. A price fall combined with a low relative strength is a bearish signal.

There were 12,893,526 shares worth $214.2 million traded today; the volume was 0.6 times the average daily trading volume of 23.1 million shares.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

2.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 329 words

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 104 words

Record $73 Billion Capital Investment Plan Through 2030 Announced

PG&E has unveiled a historic $73 billion five-year capital investment plan extending through 2030, marking a $10 billion increase over previous guidance. The strategy targets 9% annual rate base growth to support grid modernization, wildfire mitigation, and infrastructure for growing data center demand. Despite significant investment, CEO Patti Poppe projects flat or declining customer bills in 2027, achieved through sustained operational efficiencies including a 2% annual reduction in operations and maintenance costs. The plan allocates $20 billion to FERC-regulated transmission upgrades and funds distribution improvements without requiring new equity issuance, aiming to maintain investment-grade credit ratings and support at least 9% annual EPS growth.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

Fitch Ratings Upgrades PG&E to Investment Grade ('BBB-') with Stable Outlook

In September 2025, Fitch Ratings upgraded PG&E to investment-grade status ('BBB-') with a stable outlook, citing material improvements in wildfire risk mitigation and the regulatory protections afforded by Senate Bill 254. The rating agency highlighted the legislation's shift of the disallowance cap determination to the date of ignition as a key factor in reducing financial liability exposure. This upgrade signals strengthened credit fundamentals, enabling the utility to service debt at lower rates and benefit customer affordability. Fitch expects continued credit metric improvements, provided the company sustains its mitigation performance and the regulatory environment remains stable.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

Senate Bill 254 Wildfire Fund Expansion Creates $18 Billion Continuation Account and Enhanced Protections

California's enactment of Senate Bill 254 in September 2025 established an $18 billion wildfire fund continuation account, significantly refining the state's liability framework. The legislation reduces PG&E's financial exposure by relocating the disallowance cap determination to the ignition date and eliminating upfront utility contributions in favor of contingent obligations. Additionally, it lowers PG&E's annual fund contribution by approximately 25% starting in 2029. The bill also authorizes a second phase study to explore broader climate-driven risk allocation policies, demonstrating ongoing legislative commitment to utility sector financial stability.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat EPS Expectations Amid Operational Efficiency Gains

PG&E reported third-quarter 2025 core earnings per share of $0.50, exceeding analyst consensus by 16.3% despite a 2.5% revenue miss. The performance was driven largely by effective cost management, including sustained reductions in inspection and vendor costs, alongside favorable tax planning. Year-to-date earnings of $1.14 position the company to meet its full-year guidance, which forecasts 10% growth over 2024. Management introduced 2026 EPS guidance indicating continued 9% growth, supported by the extended capital plan and capital-efficient operations.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Department of Energy Closes $15 Billion Loan Guarantee for Project Polaris Grid Modernization

The Department of Energy finalized a $15 billion conditional loan guarantee for PG&E in January 2025 to finance "Project Polaris," a comprehensive grid modernization initiative. The low-cost financing covers hydropower expansion, battery storage, transmission upgrades, and virtual power plant development, with estimated customer savings of over $1 billion during the repayment period. The deal includes community benefit provisions focusing on workforce development and equity. Management views the loan as critical for addressing capacity expansion needs driven by artificial intelligence and data center growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 92 words

Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant Officially Enters Full Extended Operations Through 2030

Diablo Canyon's Unit 2 began extended operations in August 2025, joining Unit 1 to ensure the facility remains online through at least 2030. This extension addresses California's grid reliability needs and surging electricity demand from data centers, contributing approximately 20% of the state's clean energy. The plant serves as a vital economic engine for San LuisObispo County and maintains top-tier safety ratings from the NRC. While license renewal for operations through 2045 is pending state agency approvals, continued operation is projected to save ratepayers billions by avoiding costly alternative infrastructure investments.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Calistoga Resiliency Center Hybrid Microgrid Achieves Commercial Operation with 293 MWh Capacity

PG&E and Energy Vault commenced commercial operations of the Calistoga Resiliency Center in September 2025. This 293 MWh hybrid microgrid combines hydrogen fuel cells and lithium-ion batteries to provide 8.5 MW of capacity, capable of powering approximately 1,600 customers for at least 48 hours during outages. Designed to function independently from the main grid, the facility protects critical community infrastructure in a high-fire-threat area. The project represents a scalable model for zero-emissions resilience technology and is part of a broader deployment of community microgrids across PG&E's territory.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Data Center Power Demand Pipeline Grows to 10 Gigawatts Over Next Decade

PG&E's pipeline for data center power demand has nearly tripled year-over-year to 10 gigawatts, with 1.6 gigawatts in final engineering stages scheduled for operation between 2026 and 2030. The demand is concentrated in smaller, inference-model projects clustered in Northern California, capitalizing on the region's fiber infrastructure. Management estimates that every gigawatt of new load could reduce customer bills by 1-2% through rate base growth. While current capital planning remains conservative, the robust pipeline offers significant potential upside for future investment and infrastructure expansion.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 90 words

Strategic Partnership with Smart Wires Deploys Advanced Power Flow Control Technology at Los Esteros Substation

PG&E has partnered with Smart Wires to deploy advanced power flow control technology at the Los Esteros substation by late 2025. The installation of SmartValve devices will unlock over 100 MW of additional capacity in the San Jose area by mitigating thermal overloads on existing transmission lines. This solution was selected for its speed and cost-effectiveness compared to traditional infrastructure expansion, serving as a bridge until a permanent transmission line is completed in 2032. The project underscores a strategic focus on deploying innovative grid-enhancing technologies to manage rapid load growth.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 82 words

CEO Patti Poppe Secures Five-Year Contract Extension Through 2030, Ensuring Leadership Continuity

PG&E's Board extended CEO Patti Poppe's contract through 2030 to ensure leadership stability during a pivotal phase of operational recovery and capital execution. The extension aims to mitigate risks associated with executive transitions and preserve institutional knowledge essential for regulatory engagement and strategic initiatives. Poppe's tenure has been characterized by consistent cost reduction achievements, improved brand trust, and effective capital planning. The move signals Board confidence in her direction and provides long-term visibility for stakeholders regarding the company's financial and operational priorities.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 212 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix-Benton PUD Spectrum Agreement Strengthens Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Supporting PG&E's Wildfire Mitigation and Grid Hardening

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a $771,000 spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County in Washington for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license. Industry reports indicate the utility-owned network will serve a 927-square-mile territory covering more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the agreement represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, outage detection and restoration, and field workforce communications aligned with clean-energy compliance requirements.

Impact on PG&E: PCG

Following this development, PG&E Corporation, through Pacific Gas and Electric Company, gains another operational reference relevant to its private wireless investments across Northern and Central California. Operational metrics indicate PG&E serves approximately 16 million people across 70,000 square miles and holds an existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement, deploying private LTE infrastructure across high fire-threat districts.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement strengthens the ecosystem supporting PG&E's deployment, including device availability, tower partner networks, and cybersecurity frameworks. Expanded adoption also reinforces regulatory familiarity before the California Public Utilities Commission reviewing PG&E's wildfire mitigation plans and undergrounding commitments.
