May 10 2026: Sempra Shares Climb Following $10 Billion Infrastructure Stake Sale

Sempra's stock price surged nearly 5% on the New York Stock Exchange following the announcement of a significant divestiture. On 10th May 2026, the San Diego-based utility holding company revealed it would sell a 45% interest in its infrastructure business to private equity firm KKR for $10 billion. This strategic move is aimed at simplifying Sempra's business model and enhancing its financial position.

The infrastructure unit being sold includes operations related to liquefied natural gas terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra's chairman and CEO, emphasized that the transaction is designed to streamline operations and fund robust utility growth in key markets like Texas and California. He stated that this sale underscores Sempra's commitment to a long-term partnership with KKR, aligning their vision to strengthen America's global leadership in LNG exports.

Upon completion of the deal, a KKR-led consortium will hold a 65% equity stake in Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra will maintain a 25% interest and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority will acquire the remaining 10%. Sempra indicated that the transaction will result in approximately 95% of its earnings coming from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This sale marks a pivotal shift in Sempra's strategic focus, allowing the company to concentrate on its core utility operations while simultaneously bolstering its financial strength. The transaction is expected to enhance Sempra's ability to invest in its regulated utility businesses, which are crucial for growth in the evolving energy landscape. Analysts are closely watching how this move will impact Sempra's long-term growth trajectory and market positioning.

Investors are encouraged by the news, as the stock's upward movement reflects confidence in Sempra's strategic direction. The collaboration with KKR, a well-established player in the private equity space, could open new avenues for future investments and operational efficiencies. As Sempra continues to navigate the complexities of the energy market, this transaction may serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation in the utility sector.

May 09 2026: Sempra Targets 11% Utility Rate Base Growth by 2030

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company, is embarking on a strategic simplification of its business model to prioritize utility operations. This shift is expected to drive an 11% annual rate base growth through the year 2030, as announced on 11th May 2026.

A key factor contributing to Sempra's recent performance is the Oncor base rate review settlement. This settlement establishes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5% and a return on equity (ROE) of 9.75%, which aligns rates more closely with the current cost environment, thereby enhancing profitability.

Texas has emerged as a focal point for infrastructure development, driven by surging demand from artificial intelligence and data centers. In response, Sempra has initiated a record $65 billion capital plan, with a significant portion of the rate base expected to be concentrated in Texas by 2030, positioning the company favorably for future growth.

To support this ambitious capital deployment, operational modernization at Oncor is underway, which includes diversifying supply chains. Sempra has secured labor and materials through 2028 to mitigate execution risks, ensuring that the company remains on track with its development goals.

In addition, Sempra Infrastructure is poised for significant near-term revenue contributions. The first production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) at ECA Phase 1 is scheduled for next month, with substantial completion anticipated by this summer. Furthermore, management has identified approximately $9 billion in incremental capital opportunities, primarily in Texas, driven by the rapid growth of large-load customers.

value.Title: Trump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public lands

urn:contentItem:6JJK-CTM3-SCTW-63FD-00000-00Trump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public lands2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:50ZLexisNexisBUSINESS NEWSDOMESTIC NEWSPOLITICAL NEWSSTATE AND REGIONALTrump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public landsMATTHEW BROWNAssociated PressBILLINGS, Mont.

BILLINGS, Mont. — The Interior Department is canceling a rule that put conservation on equal footing with development, as President Donald Trump's administration eases restrictions on industries and seeks to boost drilling, logging, mining and grazing on taxpayer-owned land.

The 2024 rule adopted under former President Joe Biden was meant to refocus the Interior Department's Bureau of Land Management, which oversees about 10% of land in the U.S. It allowed public property to be leased for restoration in the same way that oil companies lease land for drilling.

But Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has said the rule could have blocked access to hundreds of thousands of acres (hectares) of land — preventing energy and timber production and hurting ranchers who graze on public lands.

Supporters argued that conservation had long been a secondary consideration at the land bureau, neglecting its mission under the 1976 Federal Lands Policy Management Act. While the bureau previously issued leases for conservation purposes in limited cases, it never had a dedicated program prior to the Biden administration.

Bobby McEnaney with the Natural Resources Defense Council said repealing the rule ”means less protection for the clean drinking water, less protection for endangered wildlife that depend on healthy habitat, and less accountability when corporations leave these landscapes damaged and degraded."

In documents released Monday, administration officials said it exceeded the land bureau’s authority for outside parties to be allowed to obtain conservation leases.

Industry groups and their Republican allies in Congress strongly opposed the rule and had lobbied to repeal it. They said the change under Biden violated the “multiple use” mandate for Interior Department lands by catapulting the “non-use” of federal lands — meaning restoration leases — to a position of prominence.

“This action provides greater clarity and predictability for independent oil and natural gas producers—many of whom rely on consistent permitting and leasing processes to operate efficiently and invest in domestic energy supply,” Dan Naatz with the Independent Petroleum Association of America said in a statement.

The federal government's vast land holdings are concentrated in Western states including Alaska, California, Nevada, New Mexico, Utah and Wyoming. Since taking office, Trump has pursued a flurry of actions aimed at boosting fossil fuel production from those taxpayer-owned sites. The Republican administration also has sought to sideline some renewable energy projects, claiming they were unfairly subsidized under Biden.

The repeal is effective 30 days after it is published in the Federal Register, which was scheduled for Tuesday.

It comes after Republicans in Congress in recent months canceled land management plans adopted in the closing days of Biden's administration that restricted development in large areas of Alaska, Montana and North Dakota.

In addition to its surface land holdings, the Bureau of Land Management regulates publicly owned underground mineral reserves — such as coal for power plants and lithium for renewable energy — across more than 1 million square miles (2.5 million square kilometers). The bureau has a history of industry-friendly policies and for more than a century has sold grazing permits and oil and gas leases.

urn:contentItem:6JJN-M7W3-S8VK-W2D8-00000-00Can PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), sustain its upward momentum this Tuesday?2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:05ZLexisNexisCan PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), sustain its upward momentum this Tuesday?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PG&E Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, increased 0.9% during its previous trading session to close at $16.21.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 332 words

Mon Apr 13, 2026: PG&E (PCG) Sees Analyst Price Target Adjustments Amid Wildfire Policy Changes

PG&E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) is witnessing a shift in its market narrative as analysts reassess the company's valuation amidst evolving wildfire policies. On 13th April 2026, analysts adjusted PG&E's fair value estimate to US$22.63 per share from its previous estimate of US$22.53. This slight increase reflects a complex interplay of factors impacting the utility giant, including affordability concerns and regulatory changes in California's wildfire management.

Recent commentary from market analysts presents a mixed outlook on PG&E's future. UBS upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy, setting a price target of US$23. The firm points to recent reforms in wildfire policies and ongoing affordability discussions as potential catalysts for positive performance. Moreover, UBS highlights a significant 43% price-to-earnings (P/E) discount that could narrow as risks associated with wildfire liabilities diminish over time. Barclays also raised its price target to US$23, maintaining an Overweight rating based on solid fundamentals, even as PG&E's share price has strengthened recently.

Conversely, some analysts express caution. Jefferies downgraded PG&E, suggesting the risk-return profile has become less appealing compared to earlier assessments. Morgan Stanley, while also raising its price target to US$23, emphasized political and affordability pressures that may impact the company's growth trajectory, particularly in the context of data center demand and regulatory constraints.

Adding to the complexity of PG&E's narrative is the Nuclear Regulatory Commission's recent renewal of operating licenses for its Diablo Canyon Units 1 and 2 for an additional 20 years. This decision, which extends Unit 1's license to November 2044 and Unit 2's to August 2045, reflects a commitment to long-term energy production, albeit amid ongoing scrutiny over safety and environmental impact.

As PG&E navigates this multifaceted landscape, its commitment to wildfire prevention is also taking shape. The company has partnered with Lockheed Martin, Salesforce, and Wells Fargo to form EMBERPOINT LLC, a venture focused on advanced wildfire detection and response technologies. This initiative, pending regulatory approval, underscores PG&E's proactive stance in addressing wildfire risks, which remain a critical aspect of its operational narrative.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 8.2% is lower than sector average of 9.9%. This is computed as net profit margin of 10.8% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.18 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 4.3.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

value.Title: GNW : Stifel Launches Project Finance Offering to Expand Energy and Deep Tech

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-0RV3-RV7B-D4PM-00000-00GNW : Stifel Launches Project Finance Offering to Expand Energy and Deep Tech2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisADHOC-NEWSFinancial ServicesGNW : Stifel Launches Project Finance Offering to Expand Energy and Deep Tech

Capabilities

Industry Veterans Bret Turner & Sayoji Goli Join to Lead New Effort

ST. LOUIS, May 05, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Stifel Financial Corp. (NYSE: SF) today announced the launch of a Project Finance platform to support the development of energy and infrastructure sectors and bridge the transition financing gap. The initiative is led by newly appointed Managing Directors Bret Turner and Sayoji Goli.

Turner and Goli join Stifel from First Citizens Bank and bring over a decade of collaboration and a proven track record of advancing emerging technologies and business models. Turner previously founded and scaled the project finance platform at Silicon Valley Bank, building it into one of the leading renewable energy lending businesses in the United States prior to its acquisition by First Citizens Bank.

Stifel's Project Finance platform is designed to support companies and sponsors scale by financing proven commercial technologies in established and emerging infrastructure assets with strong credit profiles. The Project Finance product expansion compliments the recent addition of Energy Tech and established Deep Tech teams supporting the next evolution of global energy production and consumption. The offering includes a range of financing solutions, including development loans, construction-to-term loans, interest rate swaps, depository services, and investment banking advisory.

"These key hires significantly enhance Stifel's ability to finance infrastructure across the energy and industrial sectors," said Chris Reichert, Stifel Bank CEO. "Their addition supports Stifel's differentiated approach of integrating Project Finance with our Venture Banking, Fund Banking, and Capital Markets capabilities, enabling support for investors and portfolio companies from early-stage development through commercialization and monetization."

"The need for reliable, affordable, and clean power, driven by rapid electrification and digitalization, is creating significant demand for new infrastructure needs that will require vast amounts of capital," said Turner. "This platform allows us to bring our experience in structuring and executing, across both established and emerging technologies, to the Stifel client base."

StifelCompanyInformation Stifel Financial Corp. (NYSE: SF) is a diversified financial services firm providing wealth management, commercial and investment banking, trading, and research services to individuals, institutions, and municipalities. Founded in 1890 and headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, the firm operates more than 400 offices across the United States and in major global financial centers. As a firm where success meets success, Stifel works closely with retail and institutional clients aiming to transform opportunities into achievement.

Stifel Bank and Stifel Bank & Trust, Members FDIC, offer a full range of consumer and commercial lending solutions.

To learn more about Stifel, please visit the Company's website at www.stifel.com. For global disclosures, please visit www.stifel.com/investor-relations/press-releases.

Media Contact:

Neil Shapiro, +1 (212) 271-3447 shapiron@stifel.com

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-CTR3-SCTW-63G9-00000-00Trump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public lands2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:50ZLexisNexisBUSINESS NEWSDOMESTIC NEWSPOLITICAL NEWSSTATE AND REGIONALTrump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public landsMATTHEW BROWNAssociated PressBILLINGS, Mont.

BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Interior Department is canceling a rule that put conservation on equal footing with development, as President Donald Trump's administration eases restrictions on industries and seeks to boost drilling, logging, mining and grazing on taxpayer-owned land.

The 2024 rule adopted under former President Joe Biden was meant to refocus the Interior Department's Bureau of Land Management, which oversees about 10% of land in the U.S. It allowed public property to be leased for restoration in the same way that oil companies lease land for drilling.

But Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has said the rule could have blocked access to hundreds of thousands of acres (hectares) of land — preventing energy and timber production and hurting ranchers who graze on public lands.

Supporters argued that conservation had long been a secondary consideration at the land bureau, neglecting its mission under the 1976 Federal Lands Policy Management Act. While the bureau previously issued leases for conservation purposes in limited cases, it never had a dedicated program prior to the Biden administration.

Bobby McEnaney with the Natural Resources Defense Council said repealing the rule ”means less protection for the clean drinking water, less protection for endangered wildlife that depend on healthy habitat, and less accountability when corporations leave these landscapes damaged and degraded."

In documents released Monday, administration officials said it exceeded the land bureau’s authority for outside parties to be allowed to obtain conservation leases.

Industry groups and their Republican allies in Congress strongly opposed the rule and had lobbied to repeal it. They said the change under Biden violated the “multiple use” mandate for Interior Department lands by catapulting the “non-use” of federal lands — meaning restoration leases — to a position of prominence.

“This action provides greater clarity and predictability for independent oil and natural gas producers—many of whom rely on consistent permitting and leasing processes to operate efficiently and invest in domestic energy supply,” Dan Naatz with the Independent Petroleum Association of America said in a statement.

The federal government's vast land holdings are concentrated in Western states including Alaska, California, Nevada, New Mexico, Utah and Wyoming. Since taking office, Trump has pursued a flurry of actions aimed at boosting fossil fuel production from those taxpayer-owned sites. The Republican administration also has sought to sideline some renewable energy projects, claiming they were unfairly subsidized under Biden.

The repeal is effective 30 days after it is published in the Federal Register, which was scheduled for Tuesday.

It comes after Republicans in Congress in recent months canceled land management plans adopted in the closing days of Biden's administration that restricted development in large areas of Alaska, Montana and North Dakota.

In addition to its surface land holdings, the Bureau of Land Management regulates publicly owned underground mineral reserves — such as coal for power plants and lithium for renewable energy — across more than 1 million square miles (2.5 million square kilometers). The bureau has a history of industry-friendly policies and for more than a century has sold grazing permits and oil and gas leases.

urn:contentItem:6JJK-CW13-SD69-R46P-00000-00Trump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public lands2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:50ZLexisNexisBUSINESS NEWSDOMESTIC NEWSPOLITICAL NEWSSTATE AND REGIONALTrump administration cancels rule that made conservation a 'use' of public landsMATTHEW BROWNAssociated PressBILLINGS, Mont.

BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Interior Department is canceling a rule that put conservation on equal footing with development, as President Donald Trump's administration eases restrictions on industries and seeks to boost drilling, logging, mining and grazing on taxpayer-owned land.

The 2024 rule adopted under former President Joe Biden was meant to refocus the Interior Department's Bureau of Land Management, which oversees about 10% of land in the U.S. It allowed public property to be leased for restoration in the same way that oil companies lease land for drilling.

But Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has said the rule could have blocked access to hundreds of thousands of acres (hectares) of land — preventing energy and timber production and hurting ranchers who graze on public lands.

Supporters argued that conservation had long been a secondary consideration at the land bureau, neglecting its mission under the 1976 Federal Lands Policy Management Act. While the bureau previously issued leases for conservation purposes in limited cases, it never had a dedicated program prior to the Biden administration.

Bobby McEnaney with the Natural Resources Defense Council said repealing the rule ”means less protection for the clean drinking water, less protection for endangered wildlife that depend on healthy habitat, and less accountability when corporations leave these landscapes damaged and degraded."

In documents released Monday, administration officials said it exceeded the land bureau’s authority for outside parties to be allowed to obtain conservation leases.

Industry groups and their Republican allies in Congress strongly opposed the rule and had lobbied to repeal it. They said the change under Biden violated the “multiple use” mandate for Interior Department lands by catapulting the “non-use” of federal lands — meaning restoration leases — to a position of prominence.

“This action provides greater clarity and predictability for independent oil and natural gas producers—many of whom rely on consistent permitting and leasing processes to operate efficiently and invest in domestic energy supply,” Dan Naatz with the Independent Petroleum Association of America said in a statement.

The federal government's vast land holdings are concentrated in Western states including Alaska, California, Nevada, New Mexico, Utah and Wyoming. Since taking office, Trump has pursued a flurry of actions aimed at boosting fossil fuel production from those taxpayer-owned sites. The Republican administration also has sought to sideline some renewable energy projects, claiming they were unfairly subsidized under Biden.

The repeal is effective 30 days after it is published in the Federal Register, which was scheduled for Tuesday.

It comes after Republicans in Congress in recent months canceled land management plans adopted in the closing days of Biden's administration that restricted development in large areas of Alaska, Montana and North Dakota.

In addition to its surface land holdings, the Bureau of Land Management regulates publicly owned underground mineral reserves — such as coal for power plants and lithium for renewable energy — across more than 1 million square miles (2.5 million square kilometers). The bureau has a history of industry-friendly policies and for more than a century has sold grazing permits and oil and gas leases.

urn:contentItem:6JJF-TTT3-SF9G-K0TM-00000-00Will PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), bounce back this Monday?2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:42:04ZLexisNexisWill PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), bounce back this Monday?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PG&E Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, decreased 0.7% during its previous trading session to close at $16.07.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

Mon Apr 13, 2026: PG&E (PCG) Sees Analyst Price Target Adjustments Amid Wildfire Policy Changes

PG&E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) is witnessing a shift in its market narrative as analysts reassess the company's valuation amidst evolving wildfire policies. On 13th April 2026, analysts adjusted PG&E's fair value estimate to US$22.63 per share from its previous estimate of US$22.53. This slight increase reflects a complex interplay of factors impacting the utility giant, including affordability concerns and regulatory changes in California's wildfire management.

Recent commentary from market analysts presents a mixed outlook on PG&E's future. UBS upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy, setting a price target of US$23. The firm points to recent reforms in wildfire policies and ongoing affordability discussions as potential catalysts for positive performance. Moreover, UBS highlights a significant 43% price-to-earnings (P/E) discount that could narrow as risks associated with wildfire liabilities diminish over time. Barclays also raised its price target to US$23, maintaining an Overweight rating based on solid fundamentals, even as PG&E's share price has strengthened recently.

Conversely, some analysts express caution. Jefferies downgraded PG&E, suggesting the risk-return profile has become less appealing compared to earlier assessments. Morgan Stanley, while also raising its price target to US$23, emphasized political and affordability pressures that may impact the company's growth trajectory, particularly in the context of data center demand and regulatory constraints.

Adding to the complexity of PG&E's narrative is the Nuclear Regulatory Commission's recent renewal of operating licenses for its Diablo Canyon Units 1 and 2 for an additional 20 years. This decision, which extends Unit 1's license to November 2044 and Unit 2's to August 2045, reflects a commitment to long-term energy production, albeit amid ongoing scrutiny over safety and environmental impact.

As PG&E navigates this multifaceted landscape, its commitment to wildfire prevention is also taking shape. The company has partnered with Lockheed Martin, Salesforce, and Wells Fargo to form EMBERPOINT LLC, a venture focused on advanced wildfire detection and response technologies. This initiative, pending regulatory approval, underscores PG&E's proactive stance in addressing wildfire risks, which remain a critical aspect of its operational narrative.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Monday, May 11 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PG&E Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, increased 0.9% during its previous trading session to close at $16.21.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

Mon Apr 13, 2026: PG&E (PCG) Sees Analyst Price Target Adjustments Amid Wildfire Policy Changes

PG&E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) is witnessing a shift in its market narrative as analysts reassess the company's valuation amidst evolving wildfire policies. On 13th April 2026, analysts adjusted PG&E's fair value estimate to US$22.63 per share from its previous estimate of US$22.53. This slight increase reflects a complex interplay of factors impacting the utility giant, including affordability concerns and regulatory changes in California's wildfire management.

Recent commentary from market analysts presents a mixed outlook on PG&E's future. UBS upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy, setting a price target of US$23. The firm points to recent reforms in wildfire policies and ongoing affordability discussions as potential catalysts for positive performance. Moreover, UBS highlights a significant 43% price-to-earnings (P/E) discount that could narrow as risks associated with wildfire liabilities diminish over time. Barclays also raised its price target to US$23, maintaining an Overweight rating based on solid fundamentals, even as PG&E's share price has strengthened recently.

Conversely, some analysts express caution. Jefferies downgraded PG&E, suggesting the risk-return profile has become less appealing compared to earlier assessments. Morgan Stanley, while also raising its price target to US$23, emphasized political and affordability pressures that may impact the company's growth trajectory, particularly in the context of data center demand and regulatory constraints.

Adding to the complexity of PG&E's narrative is the Nuclear Regulatory Commission's recent renewal of operating licenses for its Diablo Canyon Units 1 and 2 for an additional 20 years. This decision, which extends Unit 1's license to November 2044 and Unit 2's to August 2045, reflects a commitment to long-term energy production, albeit amid ongoing scrutiny over safety and environmental impact.

As PG&E navigates this multifaceted landscape, its commitment to wildfire prevention is also taking shape. The company has partnered with Lockheed Martin, Salesforce, and Wells Fargo to form EMBERPOINT LLC, a venture focused on advanced wildfire detection and response technologies. This initiative, pending regulatory approval, underscores PG&E's proactive stance in addressing wildfire risks, which remain a critical aspect of its operational narrative.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, PG&E Corporation closed lower the previous trading day. PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG), the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, decreased 0.7% during its previous trading session to close at $16.07.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a leading California-based utility provider offering electrical and natural gas services. PG&E's integral role in California's energy infrastructure is underpinned by its strategic initiatives in grid modernization and the expansion of renewable energy sources, including nuclear energy through the Diablo Canyon nuclear plant. The company is proactive in addressing wildfire liabilities with comprehensive risk management strategies and policy compliance. As industry dynamics shift towards cleaner energy, PG&E's focus on regulatory policies and safety standards strengthens its competitive positioning, enhancing long-term value for PG&E stock investors and aligning with sustainability goals.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

Mon Apr 13, 2026: PG&E (PCG) Sees Analyst Price Target Adjustments Amid Wildfire Policy Changes

PG&E Corporation (NYSE: PCG) is witnessing a shift in its market narrative as analysts reassess the company's valuation amidst evolving wildfire policies. On 13th April 2026, analysts adjusted PG&E's fair value estimate to US$22.63 per share from its previous estimate of US$22.53. This slight increase reflects a complex interplay of factors impacting the utility giant, including affordability concerns and regulatory changes in California's wildfire management.

Recent commentary from market analysts presents a mixed outlook on PG&E's future. UBS upgraded the stock from Neutral to Buy, setting a price target of US$23. The firm points to recent reforms in wildfire policies and ongoing affordability discussions as potential catalysts for positive performance. Moreover, UBS highlights a significant 43% price-to-earnings (P/E) discount that could narrow as risks associated with wildfire liabilities diminish over time. Barclays also raised its price target to US$23, maintaining an Overweight rating based on solid fundamentals, even as PG&E's share price has strengthened recently.

Conversely, some analysts express caution. Jefferies downgraded PG&E, suggesting the risk-return profile has become less appealing compared to earlier assessments. Morgan Stanley, while also raising its price target to US$23, emphasized political and affordability pressures that may impact the company's growth trajectory, particularly in the context of data center demand and regulatory constraints.

Adding to the complexity of PG&E's narrative is the Nuclear Regulatory Commission's recent renewal of operating licenses for its Diablo Canyon Units 1 and 2 for an additional 20 years. This decision, which extends Unit 1's license to November 2044 and Unit 2's to August 2045, reflects a commitment to long-term energy production, albeit amid ongoing scrutiny over safety and environmental impact.

As PG&E navigates this multifaceted landscape, its commitment to wildfire prevention is also taking shape. The company has partnered with Lockheed Martin, Salesforce, and Wells Fargo to form EMBERPOINT LLC, a venture focused on advanced wildfire detection and response technologies. This initiative, pending regulatory approval, underscores PG&E's proactive stance in addressing wildfire risks, which remain a critical aspect of its operational narrative.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

NYSE END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

SAN FRANCISCO, Monday, May 11 2026 - PG&E Corporation's (NYSE: PCG $16.20) stock price increased 13.0c (0.8%) from its previous trading session to close at $16.20.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 7.9 points (0.1%) in the day, the relative price increase was 0.7%.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

PG&E Corporation, a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates as a major energy utility provider in California through its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric Company. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity from diverse sources, including natural gas, hydroelectric, nuclear, and increasingly renewables, alongside the transportation and delivery of natural gas to residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Northern and Central California. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, driven by California's aggressive clean energy targets, as well as grid modernization initiatives like advanced metering infrastructure and support for electric vehicle charging networks. These areas are fueled by rising demand for sustainable solutions, regulatory incentives, and technological advancements, positioning PG&E for long-term growth in a transitioning energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) operates as a Pacific Gas & Electric stock, recognized for its PG&E regulated utility services, providing PG&E electric service California and PG&E natural gas service. As a major player in the region, the PG&E stock is often scrutinized for its PG&E earnings, PG&E dividend, and PG&E stock forecast. With a PG&E market cap reflecting its significant presence, the company's ongoing PG&E safety program and PG&E ESG initiatives aim to enhance its reputation and tackle PG&E wildfire mitigation alongside PG&E wildfire liabilities. Following PG&E bankruptcy exit and PG&E legal settlements, the PCG investment thesis has been cautiously optimistic. Recent PCG analyst update and PG&E analyst rating influence the PG&E price target, with the BMO price target PG&E being a key indicator. Investors also focus on PG&E price target BMO Capital as part of PG&E investment strategies. Continuous PG&E infrastructure upgrades, including PG&E grid modernization and PG&E smart meter implementation, bolster its operational resilience, aided by systematic PG&E power outage updates. Consequently, the latest PG&E shareholder update features strategic insights on PG&E rate case adjustments.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: PG&E Secures NRC License Renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant

PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) has received a significant boost as it secured approval from the federal Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for a 20-year license renewal for its Diablo Canyon Power Plant. This approval, announced on April 2, 2026, comes after a thorough review process that confirmed the facility's safety and environmental soundness for continued operations until 2046. However, extending operations beyond 2030 will require further action from the California Legislature.

The NRC's endorsement marks a critical milestone for PG&E, especially as the company navigates complex regulatory landscapes and market dynamics. On 15th April 2026, Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith downgraded PG&E Corporation from Buy to Hold, adjusting the price target to $19 from $20. This decision stems from diminishing confidence in wildfire liability reforms in California, with Dumoulin-Smith noting that alignment among utilities and insurers remains tenuous, casting uncertainty over potential political support for structural changes.

In contrast, JPMorgan has taken a more optimistic stance on PG&E Corporation, raising its price target on the shares to $24 from a previous $21 while maintaining an Overweight rating. The bank's revised outlook reflects updates to its models for the North America utilities sector, suggesting a more favorable view of PG&E's financial trajectory amidst ongoing challenges.

PG&E Corporation, which provides electricity and natural gas services across California, faces a dual challenge of operational regulatory compliance and market perceptions. While the recent NRC approval is a positive development, the company must also address investor concerns about wildfire risk and related liabilities. This complex environment necessitates a careful navigation of both operational and legislative hurdles to ensure long-term viability.

As PG&E Corporation moves forward, investors will be closely watching not just the implications of the Diablo Canyon license renewal, but also the broader context of California's regulatory framework and the evolving landscape of utility operations. While there is notable potential for growth and stability within PG&E, some analysts point to alternative investment opportunities, particularly in the AI sector, which may present greater upside and reduced risk.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 104 words

Record $73 Billion Capital Investment Plan Through 2030 Announced

PG&E has unveiled a historic $73 billion five-year capital investment plan extending through 2030, marking a $10 billion increase over previous guidance. The strategy targets 9% annual rate base growth to support grid modernization, wildfire mitigation, and infrastructure for growing data center demand. Despite significant investment, CEO Patti Poppe projects flat or declining customer bills in 2027, achieved through sustained operational efficiencies including a 2% annual reduction in operations and maintenance costs. The plan allocates $20 billion to FERC-regulated transmission upgrades and funds distribution improvements without requiring new equity issuance, aiming to maintain investment-grade credit ratings and support at least 9% annual EPS growth.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

Fitch Ratings Upgrades PG&E to Investment Grade ('BBB-') with Stable Outlook

In September 2025, Fitch Ratings upgraded PG&E to investment-grade status ('BBB-') with a stable outlook, citing material improvements in wildfire risk mitigation and the regulatory protections afforded by Senate Bill 254. The rating agency highlighted the legislation's shift of the disallowance cap determination to the date of ignition as a key factor in reducing financial liability exposure. This upgrade signals strengthened credit fundamentals, enabling the utility to service debt at lower rates and benefit customer affordability. Fitch expects continued credit metric improvements, provided the company sustains its mitigation performance and the regulatory environment remains stable.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

Senate Bill 254 Wildfire Fund Expansion Creates $18 Billion Continuation Account and Enhanced Protections

California's enactment of Senate Bill 254 in September 2025 established an $18 billion wildfire fund continuation account, significantly refining the state's liability framework. The legislation reduces PG&E's financial exposure by relocating the disallowance cap determination to the ignition date and eliminating upfront utility contributions in favor of contingent obligations. Additionally, it lowers PG&E's annual fund contribution by approximately 25% starting in 2029. The bill also authorizes a second phase study to explore broader climate-driven risk allocation policies, demonstrating ongoing legislative commitment to utility sector financial stability.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat EPS Expectations Amid Operational Efficiency Gains

PG&E reported third-quarter 2025 core earnings per share of $0.50, exceeding analyst consensus by 16.3% despite a 2.5% revenue miss. The performance was driven largely by effective cost management, including sustained reductions in inspection and vendor costs, alongside favorable tax planning. Year-to-date earnings of $1.14 position the company to meet its full-year guidance, which forecasts 10% growth over 2024. Management introduced 2026 EPS guidance indicating continued 9% growth, supported by the extended capital plan and capital-efficient operations.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Department of Energy Closes $15 Billion Loan Guarantee for Project Polaris Grid Modernization

The Department of Energy finalized a $15 billion conditional loan guarantee for PG&E in January 2025 to finance "Project Polaris," a comprehensive grid modernization initiative. The low-cost financing covers hydropower expansion, battery storage, transmission upgrades, and virtual power plant development, with estimated customer savings of over $1 billion during the repayment period. The deal includes community benefit provisions focusing on workforce development and equity. Management views the loan as critical for addressing capacity expansion needs driven by artificial intelligence and data center growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 92 words

Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant Officially Enters Full Extended Operations Through 2030

Diablo Canyon's Unit 2 began extended operations in August 2025, joining Unit 1 to ensure the facility remains online through at least 2030. This extension addresses California's grid reliability needs and surging electricity demand from data centers, contributing approximately 20% of the state's clean energy. The plant serves as a vital economic engine for San LuisObispo County and maintains top-tier safety ratings from the NRC. While license renewal for operations through 2045 is pending state agency approvals, continued operation is projected to save ratepayers billions by avoiding costly alternative infrastructure investments.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Calistoga Resiliency Center Hybrid Microgrid Achieves Commercial Operation with 293 MWh Capacity

PG&E and Energy Vault commenced commercial operations of the Calistoga Resiliency Center in September 2025. This 293 MWh hybrid microgrid combines hydrogen fuel cells and lithium-ion batteries to provide 8.5 MW of capacity, capable of powering approximately 1,600 customers for at least 48 hours during outages. Designed to function independently from the main grid, the facility protects critical community infrastructure in a high-fire-threat area. The project represents a scalable model for zero-emissions resilience technology and is part of a broader deployment of community microgrids across PG&E's territory.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 83 words

Data Center Power Demand Pipeline Grows to 10 Gigawatts Over Next Decade

PG&E's pipeline for data center power demand has nearly tripled year-over-year to 10 gigawatts, with 1.6 gigawatts in final engineering stages scheduled for operation between 2026 and 2030. The demand is concentrated in smaller, inference-model projects clustered in Northern California, capitalizing on the region's fiber infrastructure. Management estimates that every gigawatt of new load could reduce customer bills by 1-2% through rate base growth. While current capital planning remains conservative, the robust pipeline offers significant potential upside for future investment and infrastructure expansion.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 90 words

Strategic Partnership with Smart Wires Deploys Advanced Power Flow Control Technology at Los Esteros Substation

PG&E has partnered with Smart Wires to deploy advanced power flow control technology at the Los Esteros substation by late 2025. The installation of SmartValve devices will unlock over 100 MW of additional capacity in the San Jose area by mitigating thermal overloads on existing transmission lines. This solution was selected for its speed and cost-effectiveness compared to traditional infrastructure expansion, serving as a bridge until a permanent transmission line is completed in 2032. The project underscores a strategic focus on deploying innovative grid-enhancing technologies to manage rapid load growth.

CEO Patti Poppe Secures Five-Year Contract Extension Through 2030, Ensuring Leadership Continuity

PG&E's Board extended CEO Patti Poppe's contract through 2030 to ensure leadership stability during a pivotal phase of operational recovery and capital execution. The extension aims to mitigate risks associated with executive transitions and preserve institutional knowledge essential for regulatory engagement and strategic initiatives. Poppe's tenure has been characterized by consistent cost reduction achievements, improved brand trust, and effective capital planning. Apr 22, 2026: Anterix-Benton PUD Spectrum Agreement Strengthens Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Supporting PG&E's Wildfire Mitigation and Grid Hardening

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a $771,000 spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County in Washington for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license. Industry reports indicate the utility-owned network will serve a 927-square-mile territory covering more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the agreement represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, outage detection and restoration, and field workforce communications aligned with clean-energy compliance requirements.

Impact on PG&E: PCG

Following this development, PG&E Corporation, through Pacific Gas and Electric Company, gains another operational reference relevant to its private wireless investments across Northern and Central California. Operational metrics indicate PG&E serves approximately 16 million people across 70,000 square miles and holds an existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement, deploying private LTE infrastructure across high fire-threat districts.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement strengthens the ecosystem supporting PG&E's deployment, including device availability, tower partner networks, and cybersecurity frameworks. Expanded adoption also reinforces regulatory familiarity before the California Public Utilities Commission reviewing PG&E's wildfire mitigation plans and undergrounding commitments.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 280 words

Apr 13, 2026: Oregon Appeals Court Reverses PacifiCorp Wildfire Verdict, Introducing New Legal Dynamics for PG&E's Post-Bankruptcy Wildfire Liability Management

On April 8, 2026, the Oregon Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Berkshire Hathaway Energy's PacifiCorp subsidiary in the James class-action case tied to Oregon's 2020 Labor Day wildfires. A three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The ruling reverses and remands the case back to the trial court.

According to financial estimates, Berkshire has projected the cost could reach tens of billions of dollars, including $1.1 billion awarded in a series of already-completed trials. The decision could pause more than 160 damages trials scheduled through 2027. Separately, PacifiCorp has agreed to pay over $2 billion to settle claims, including $575 million to the federal government. Plaintiffs' counsel has called the ruling a procedural setback and may appeal to the Oregon Supreme Court.

Impact on PG&E Corp: PCG

Following this news, PG&E Corp faces a notable shift in the legal landscape for utility wildfire litigation. PG&E emerged from bankruptcy in 2020 after its equipment was found responsible for the 2018 Camp Fire and other catastrophic blazes, having settled over $25 billion in wildfire claims.

The Oregon appellate ruling's procedural holding that class-wide evidence assumptions are impermissible across distinct wildfire events could have influenced PG&E's own litigation trajectory had it been established earlier. Going forward, this precedent benefits PG&E's ongoing wildfire risk management, as the utility continues to operate infrastructure in extremely fire-prone regions of Northern and Central California and remains exposed to future litigation from any equipment-related fire ignitions.

www.buysellsignals.com

Sempra (NYSE:SRE $91.53), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, has decreased $3.14 (or 3.3%) in the past week to close at $91.53. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 168.8 points (or 2.3%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -5.7%.

During the week its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 6.3% was 1 time its average weekly volatility of 4.4%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $2.1 billion to $59.8 billion.

The volume was 0.9 times average trading per week of 18.4 million shares.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 346 words

Sat Apr 18, 2026: Sempra Secures Regulatory Approval for Oncor's Rate Review

Sempra has announced a significant regulatory milestone as the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT) approved a final order in Oncor Electric Delivery Company LLC's comprehensive base rate review proceeding. This approval, dated 20th April 2026, secures an annual revenue requirement of approximately $6.97 billion for Oncor, in which Sempra holds an 80.25% interest, marking an increase of about $560 million, or 8.7%, over Oncor's adjusted annualized revenues as outlined in the rate application.

The approved order includes a revised regulatory capital structure ratio of 56.5% debt to 43.5% equity, an authorized return on equity of 9.75%, and an authorized cost of debt of 4.94%. These figures indicate a positive shift from Oncor's previous authorized capital structure, which was 57.5% debt to 42.5% equity, along with a return on equity of 9.70% and a cost of debt of 4.39%. This change is expected to bolster Oncor's financial health, benefiting both Sempra and its investors.

Additionally, the order establishes a self-insurance reserve with an annual accrual amount in rates of $200 million for storm costs and other self-insured losses, a notable increase from the previous $122 million. A five-year amortization period for applicable regulatory assets and liabilities has also been set, excluding rate case expenses and year-end 2024 deferred system resiliency plan costs.

Oncor anticipates that this regulatory approval will have a positive impact on its future earnings, cash flow, and credit metrics. The company is permitted to surcharge the difference between the new billing rates and its current rates for the period from January 1, 2026, until the new rates take effect, which will be 45 days from the date of the final order. Oncor plans to file for this surcharge shortly after the new billing rates become effective, with recovery expected throughout 2026.

This development is a significant step forward for Sempra and Oncor, enhancing their financial stability and positioning them for future growth in the energy sector. Investors may want to evaluate the implications of this approval on Sempra's profitability and stock valuations, as understanding the business dynamics is crucial before making investment decisions.

According to industry reports, American Electric Power lifted its five-year capital plan to $78 billion from $72 billion on May 5, 2026, citing accelerating contracted data center demand through 2030. The $6 billion expansion funds new transmission projects in SPP ($1.6 billion) and PJM ($1.9 billion), plus new gas-fired generation in Indiana, while contracted load rose to 63 GW with nearly 90% tied to hyperscalers.

Operational metrics indicate that AEP Texas alone accounts for 41 GW of large-load commitments, supported by the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The plan reinforces a structural shift across US investor-owned utilities, where regulated rate base is expanding sharply to serve hyperscale and AI-driven load. AEP also raised its long-term operating earnings CAGR target to greater than 9% through 2030 and identified over $10 billion in additional line-of-sight investments beyond the current plan.

Impact on Sempra: SRE

Following this news, Sempra faces validation of its Texas-centric infrastructure thesis as AEP highlights ERCOT's 41 GW of contracted load commitments and the upcoming rollout of Texas Senate Bill 6. The bill is expected to provide greater certainty around interconnection timing for new load growth, a development that benefits Sempra's Oncor subsidiary, the largest transmission and distribution utility in Texas by customer count. Oncor's role in delivering power to data center, industrial, and residential customers across north and west Texas places Sempra in direct economic alignment with AEP Texas's load growth, since both utilities depend on shared ERCOT transmission planning, generation interconnection queues, and supply chain capacity.

Sempra's California utilities and LNG infrastructure businesses also benefit from the same secular electrification and AI-driven demand trends. Strategically, AEP's disclosure provides Sempra additional regulatory and investor support for its own multi-year Texas-led capital expansion.
